CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF SEP-02-2011 CONTINUED TO OCT-14-2011 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CHRISTINE CHANG'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION "BANE ACT VIOLATION". THE COURT DENIED PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PROVIDE A BASIS FOR RECONSIDERATION UNDER CCP 1008. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: RHONDA AQUILINA, CSR 	956
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE MEDIATION SET FOR FEB-08-2012 AT 2:00 PM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT
NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL, JURY TRIAL SET FOR MAY-07-2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON OCT-14-2011 IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
REPLY IN SUPPORT OF THEIR DISCOVERY MTN TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO DISCOVERY AND IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT GAMP, JANE L. O'HARA SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL

DISCOVERY 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS JANE GAMP AND SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVEY AND IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS - THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. ALL OBJECTIONS OVERRULED WITH THE EXCEPTION OF THE ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT OBJECTIONS RAISED IN RESPONSE TO DEFENDANT'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NUMBERS 1 - 35. WITHIN 21 DAYS, PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S INTERROGATORIES, PRODUCE ALL NON-PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS AND A COMPLETE PRIVILEGE LOG WITH RESPECT TO THOSE DOCUMENTS AS TO WHICH THE ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE IS ASSERTED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO PAY $1,200 IN SANCTIONS TO DEFENDANT WITHIN 21 DAYS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JAMES MCBRIDE; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JANE L. O'HARA GAMP AND SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANTS DEMURRER TO THE 3RD, 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF, AS A MATTER OF LAW, CANNOT SUPPORT CLAIMS FOR BREACH OF STATUTORY DUTY, DENIAL OF CONSTITUTIONAL RIGHTS OR BANE ACT VIOLATIONS. IN ADDITION, THE COURT DENIED PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO AMEND AND DENIED PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions Of Dismissal With Prejudice, Striking The Complaint, Or Entering Default Judgment Against Pltf; Or, In The Alternative, 2) Compelling Pltf'S Compliance With The Court'S Order Granting Defts' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Imposition Of Monetary ($1,620), Issue, And/Or Evidence Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances - Plaintiff Christine Chang appearing in pro per (510-523-4730).
Atty. Eleanor W. Knight of Wilson Elser Moskowitz Edelman & Dicker LLP, (415-625-9256)appearing for defendants.

Discussed - DEFENDANTS JANE GAMP, SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Of Dismissal With Prejudice, Striking The Complaint, Or Entering Default Judgment Against Pltf; Or, In The Alternative, 2) Compelling Pltf'S Compliance With The Court'S Order Granting Defts' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Imposition Of Monetary ($1,620), Issue, And/Or Evidence Sanctions. Parties stipulated to have matter decided by Judge Pro Tem.
Parties agreed to 1. Form interrogatories. 2. Written responses to request for productio of documents. 3. All responses to admission of of genuiness of documents. 4. All responses to admissions to be produced and served on defendants within 30 days. Order signed in open court. Robert Kane Judge Pro Tem, not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Respopnses To Discoveyr And Imposition Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-28-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - CHRISTINE CHANG, TEL. NO. (510) 523-4730, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. ELEANOR W. KNIGHT OF WILSON ELSER LLP, TEL. NO. 625-9256 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS JANE GAMP AND SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovey And Imposition Of Sanctions - The motion to compel is granted. All objections overruled with the exception of the attorney work product objections raised in response to Defendant's requests for production numbers 1 - 35. Within 21 days, Plaintiff shall serve full and complete responses to Defendant's interrogatories, produce all non-privileged documents and a complete privilege log with respect to those documents as to which the attorney work product doctrine is asserted. Defendant's motion for sanctions is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to pay $1,200 in sanctions to Defendant within 21 days. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: James Mcbride; Not reported. = (JJM/302)

Off Calendar Aug-25-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CHRISTINE CHANG'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION "BANE ACT VIOLATION".
Appearances: Eleanor W. Knight of Wilson Elser (625.9256) for defendants and responding parties; Christine Chang (510.523.4730) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona.
The court denied plaintiff's request for a continuance. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: plaintiff's motion for reconsideration is denied. Plaintiff does not provide a basis for reconsideration under CCP 1008. Defendant to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Rhonda Aquilina, CSR 956

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS JANE L. O'HARA GAMP AND SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Christine Chang, 510/523-4730 pltf & opposing party in propria persona; Eleanor W. Knight, Esq. of Wilson Elser Moskowitz Edelman & Dicker LLP 625-9256 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendants demurrer to the 3rd, 4th and 5th causes of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff, as a matter of law, cannot support claims for breach of statutory duty, denial of constitutional rights or Bane Act violations. In addition, the Court denied plaintiff's request for leave to amend and denied plaintiff's motion to strike. Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
